content.sanskrit: मा निषाद प्रतिष्ठां त्वमगमः शाश्वतीः समाः
content.transliteration: mā niṣāda pratiṣṭhāṁ tvamagamaḥ śāśvatīḥ samāḥ
content.text: O hunter, may you find no peace or stability for endless years to come. You have slain one of the pair of sweet-voiced Krauncha birds while they were deeply in love. This spontaneous utterance marked the birth of classical Sanskrit poetry.

content.sanskrit: धर्मात्मा रामो उवाच वचनम् हृष्टः निशाम्य च।
content.transliteration: dharmātmā rāmo uvāca vacanam hṛṣṭaḥ niśāmya ca
content.text: The righteous Lord Rama spoke concerning the profound words of counsel, with a joyful heart, after observing all the auspicious signs around him. This brought peace to everyone who was present there. May his radiant joy inspire deep calm in our hearts as well.

content.sanskrit: धर्मात्मा रामो उवाच वनम् क्रोधतः महासत्त्वः।
content.transliteration: dharmātmā rāmo uvāca vanam krodhataḥ mahāsattvaḥ
content.text: The righteous Lord Rama spoke concerning the sprawling and sacred forest, with righteous indignation, showcasing his immense strength and unyielding spirit. He remained fully committed to upholding cosmic order at all costs. This noble act restored balance and courage throughout the realm.

content.sanskrit: धर्मात्मा रामो उवाच राक्षसम् शोकार्तः अरिमर्दनः।
content.transliteration: dharmātmā rāmo uvāca rākṣasam śokārtaḥ arimardanaḥ
content.text: The righteous Lord Rama spoke concerning the fierce demon threat, though deeply afflicted by grief, standing as the ultimate subduer of all negative forces. He demonstrated that duty and righteousness always transcend personal sorrow. His calm resilience serves as a eternal beacon of hope.

content.sanskrit: धर्मात्मा रामो उवाच लङ्काम् पुनः प्लवङ्गमः।
content.transliteration: dharmātmā rāmo uvāca laṅkām punaḥ plavaṅgamaḥ
content.text: The righteous Lord Rama spoke concerning the golden city of Lanka, once again, in the loyal presence of the devoted monkey guides. Together, they prepared to face the upcoming spiritual and physical challenges. This beautiful union represents the power of collaborative faith and devotion.

content.sanskrit: धर्मात्मा रामो उवाच अयोध्याम् तदा नराधिपः।
content.transliteration: dharmātmā rāmo uvāca ayodhyām tadā narādhipaḥ
content.text: The righteous Lord Rama spoke concerning his beloved kingdom of Ayodhya, at that auspicious time, acting as the rightful sovereign and leader of men. He ruled not with authority, but with deep love and compassionate care for his subjects. His gentle presence brought prosperity and safety to all.

content.sanskrit: धर्मात्मा रामो उवाच सागरम् शीघ्रम् रघूत्तमः।
content.transliteration: dharmātmā rāmo uvāca sāgaram śīghram raghūttamaḥ
content.text: The righteous Lord Rama spoke concerning the vast and roaring ocean, with great swiftness, acting as the most distinguished descendant of the Raghu dynasty. He moved with grace and a clear sense of divine purpose. This swift action removed all obstacles and brought immediate relief to the distressed.

content.sanskrit: धर्मात्मा रामो उवाच पर्वतम् सर्वदा कपिञ्जरः।
content.transliteration: dharmātmā rāmo uvāca parvatam sarvadā kapiñjaraḥ
content.text: The righteous Lord Rama spoke concerning the majestic mountain peak, in the constant and faithful company of the noble monkey chief Kapinjara. Their enduring friendship exemplified the power of loyalty, trust, and shared spiritual goals. Together they marched forward to execute the divine mission.

content.sanskrit: धर्मात्मा रामो उवाच रामम् सत्येन राक्षसेश्वरः।
content.transliteration: dharmātmā rāmo uvāca rāmam satyena rākṣaseśvaraḥ
content.text: The righteous Lord Rama spoke concerning his own inner divine path and duty, with absolute truthfulness, while even the formidable king of the demons stood observing in silent awe. His uncompromising commitment to truth disarmed all deceit and hostility. Truth indeed remains the ultimate shield in the three worlds.

content.sanskrit: धर्मात्मा रामो उवाच सीताम् धर्मेण महानुभावः।
content.transliteration: dharmātmā rāmo uvāca sītām dharmeṇa mahānubhāvaḥ
content.text: The righteous Lord Rama spoke concerning his beloved and virtuous consort Sita, in perfect accordance with the laws of dharma, behaving as a high-souled and noble being. His every gesture breathed spiritual wisdom and moral integrity. He showed the entire world how to live a life of selfless duty.

content.sanskrit: धर्मात्मा रामो उवाच रावणम् भक्त्या वानरर्षभः।
content.transliteration: dharmātmā rāmo uvāca rāvaṇam bhaktyā vānaraṛṣabhaḥ
content.text: The righteous Lord Rama spoke concerning the formidable demon king Ravana, with deep devotion, while the heroic chief of the monkeys stood by with folded hands. This sacred bond of love and service became the foundation of their ultimate victory. It reminds us of the power of complete surrender to the divine.

content.sanskrit: धर्मात्मा रामो उवाच वानरान् वेगेन नरोत्तमः।
content.transliteration: dharmātmā rāmo uvāca vānarān vegena narottamaḥ
content.text: The righteous Lord Rama spoke concerning the devoted host of vanaras (monkeys), with swift and powerful momentum, acting as the best and most refined among human beings. He showed unmatched skill and composure under pressure. His resolute action inspired the entire host to follow the path of courage.

content.sanskrit: धर्मात्मा रामो उवाच धनुः अतीव राजीवलोचनः।
content.transliteration: dharmātmā rāmo uvāca dhanuḥ atīva rājīvalocanaḥ
content.text: The righteous Lord Rama spoke concerning the heavy, celestial bow, exceedingly well, appearing as the lotus-eyed Lord of the universe. His merciful glance brought immense comfort to all who looked upon his beautiful form. That divine vision removed all sorrow and doubts in an instant.

content.sanskrit: धर्मात्मा रामो उवाच बाणम् मुदा लक्ष्मणपूर्वजः।
content.transliteration: dharmātmā rāmo uvāca bāṇam mudā lakṣmaṇapūrvajaḥ
content.text: The righteous Lord Rama spoke concerning the swift and infallible arrow, with immense joy, acting as the beloved elder brother of Lakshmana. The deep fraternal love they shared was a beautiful testament to familial virtue and harmony. Their mutual trust remains a guiding light for household relationships.

content.sanskrit: धर्मात्मा रामो उवाच रथम् हर्षात् राक्षसान्तकः।
content.transliteration: dharmātmā rāmo uvāca ratham harṣāt rākṣasāntakaḥ
content.text: The righteous Lord Rama spoke concerning the glorious golden chariot, with profound joy, manifesting as the destroyer of all negative and demonic forces. He dispelled the darkness of fear and brought absolute freedom to the sages. His victory is a promise of protection to all who seek shelter.

content.sanskrit: धर्मात्मा रामो उवाच सैन्यम् भयात् नृपात्मजः।
content.transliteration: dharmātmā rāmo uvāca sainyam bhayāt nṛpātmajaḥ
content.text: The righteous Lord Rama spoke concerning the brave and loyal army, reassuringly to dispel all fear, behaving as the noble prince of Ayodhya. He stood as a towering shield of safety for his people, driving away all anxieties. His presence filled the entire assembly with confidence and peace.

content.sanskrit: धर्मात्मा रामो जगाम वचनम् हृष्टः निशाम्य च।
content.transliteration: dharmātmā rāmo jagāma vacanam hṛṣṭaḥ niśāmya ca
content.text: The righteous Lord Rama set out towards the profound words of counsel, with a joyful heart, after observing all the auspicious signs around him. This brought peace to everyone who was present there. May his radiant joy inspire deep calm in our hearts as well.

content.sanskrit: धर्मात्मा रामो जगाम वनम् क्रोधतः महासत्त्वः।
content.transliteration: dharmātmā rāmo jagāma vanam krodhataḥ mahāsattvaḥ
content.text: The righteous Lord Rama set out towards the sprawling and sacred forest, with righteous indignation, showcasing his immense strength and unyielding spirit. He remained fully committed to upholding cosmic order at all costs. This noble act restored balance and courage throughout the realm.

content.sanskrit: धर्मात्मा रामो जगाम राक्षसम् शोकार्तः अरिमर्दनः।
content.transliteration: dharmātmā rāmo jagāma rākṣasam śokārtaḥ arimardanaḥ
content.text: The righteous Lord Rama set out towards the fierce demon threat, though deeply afflicted by grief, standing as the ultimate subduer of all negative forces. He demonstrated that duty and righteousness always transcend personal sorrow. His calm resilience serves as a eternal beacon of hope.

content.sanskrit: धर्मात्मा रामो जगाम लङ्काम् पुनः प्लवङ्गमः।
content.transliteration: dharmātmā rāmo jagāma laṅkām punaḥ plavaṅgamaḥ
content.text: The righteous Lord Rama set out towards the golden city of Lanka, once again, in the loyal presence of the devoted monkey guides. Together, they prepared to face the upcoming spiritual and physical challenges. This beautiful union represents the power of collaborative faith and devotion.
